Asset Class Coverage.evidence: 6/18 coefficient coverage (33.3%) – commercial real estate (1/1, S1&S2+S3 in 2025) + listed equity/corporate bonds (3/6, S1&S2 only, PCAF database DQS 4) + sovereign debt (0.5/2, basic S1&S2 via PPP-GDP, no LULUCF/S3) + project finance (1/2, N/A CRE specialist) + mortgages (0.25/0.5, runoff portfolio excluded) + motor vehicle (0.25/0.5, N/A); corporate loans to financial institutions (~€5.8 bn) not covered in PCAF reporting

Data Quality Score.evidence: Average PCAF DQS 3.3 as at 31 Dec 2025, weighted by financed emissions for the first time (previously unweighted); CREF portfolio DQS ranges from 2.0–4.0 by country, Treasury portfolio DQS 4 (database factors)

Attribution Methodology.evidence: PCAF-aligned methodology per covered asset class: CREF uses loan/property value attribution factor (closely based on PCAF but explicitly noted as not fully compliant); Treasury uses EVIC for corporate bonds and PPP-adjusted GDP for sovereign. Active PCAF member (D/A/CH/LI network, CRE subgroup) and published dedicated PCAF Financed Emissions Report for FY2024. Does not cover all asset classes.

Portfolio Coverage.evidence: CREF portfolio €34.0 bn + Treasury (sovereign + corporate bonds ~€8-10 bn) = ~€42-44 bn covered out of ~€47.8 bn total assets (~€44 bn investable); loans to financial institutions (~€5.8 bn) excluded; estimated coverage ~85-90%

Temporal Coverage.evidence: 3 years of CREF financed emissions (2023: 639,681 t CO2; 2024: 607,197 t CO2; 2025: 632,812 t CO2) with carbon intensity trends + 2030 target (33 kg CO2/m²) and 2050 corridor (1–6 kg CO2/m²) from formal transition plan

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading of CRFD 2024 + Sustainability Datasheet 2024] 6.0/18 = 33.3%. Credit & equities S1&S2 (datasheet 3.4 R7, £147.8bn, 6.7M tCO2e). Infrastructure debt S1&S2 (3.4 R8, £9.0bn). Direct real estate S1+S2+S3 (3.4 R9, £6.2bn, Deepki/CRREM). CRE mortgages S1&S2 (3.4 R10, £6.3bn). Equity release mortgages S1&S2 (3.4 R11, £10.4bn). Sovereign S1 production (3.5 R21, 8.1M tCO2e, PRIMAP-hist). S3 of investees NOT included (L2053: 'We do not include investments' Scope 3 emissions'). Business loans/unlisted equity: not covered. Motor vehicle: not covered.

Data Quality Score.evidence: [Verified from Sustainability Datasheet 2024, sheet 3.5 R18] Total PCAF DQS = 2.5 (2024), 2.4 (2023). Per asset class: Credit & equities 2.2, Infrastructure debt 3.6, Direct real estate 3.8, CRE mortgages 3.2, Equity release 4.3, Sovereign 2.3. Score 3: DQS 2.5 falls in 2.5-3.5 range. Note: auto-scan found 2.3 which is sovereign DQS only, not the total.

Attribution Methodology.evidence: [Verified from CRFD 2024, L817-818] PCAF Member and standing member of Climate Data Working Group. Participating in 5 PCAF working groups in 2024. Verbatim: 'We are a PCAF Member and standing member of the Climate Data Working Group'. PCAF signatory. Uses PCAF methodology across all covered asset classes with Carbon Trust support for infra/CRE (L3589). Part A status: reported. Score 4.

Portfolio Coverage.evidence: [Verified from CRFD 2024 + Datasheet 2024] 72% AUM coverage of balance sheet assets (datasheet 3.6 R14: £225.4bn / £313.0bn). 57% of total AUM including third-party (3.6 R15). 95% emissions data coverage within included asset classes (L2023). The 23% at L2147 refers to the percentage of credit & equities portfolio from top 4 sectors by emissions, NOT portfolio coverage. Score 3 (60-80%).

Temporal Coverage.evidence: [Verified from CRFD 2024 + Datasheet] Financed emissions tracked 2022-2024 (datasheet 3.4 R7-R12). 2019 baseline for WACI targets. 64% reduction achieved vs 2019 (datasheet 3.4 R16). 2030 ambition: 60% ECI reduction. Net zero by 2040. Score 3.

Data Quality Score.evidence: [Verified from full reading] No PCAF DQS — PCAF not used. GHG emissions calculated using GHG Protocol with ecoinvent, CDP, CEDA, IEA, DEFRA factors (L3404-3411). No data quality score for financed emissions. Score 0.

Attribution Methodology.evidence: [Verified from full reading] PCAF is not mentioned anywhere in the report. Confirmed: Non-signatory (no mention). Deutsche Börse uses GHG Protocol for operational emissions, SBTi for targets (L3297). Category 15 calculated using GHG Protocol, not PCAF methodology. Score 0.

Portfolio Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading] No portfolio coverage concept applicable. Deutsche Börse's Category 15 (1,396 tCO2e) is 1.1% of total GHG emissions (L3440). No investment portfolio coverage rate disclosed or relevant. Score 0.

Temporal Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading] GHG emissions reported for 2022 (base year) and 2024 (L3440). SBTi targets: -42% S1&S2 by 2030, net zero by 2045 (L3297-3302). Category 15 data for 2 years only. Score 2: 2 years + targets.

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading of Sustainability Report 2025, 4924 lines] 14.0/18 = 77.8%. Treasury book: listed equities + corporate bonds S1&S2+S3 (L1867-1874, CHF 10.4bn measured, 68% coverage). Lending book: corporate loans S1&S2+S3 (L2607+). Mortgage book: residential RE + commercial RE (L2521-2560, CHF 8.3bn, 97% coverage). Sovereign debt: production emissions excl/incl LULUCF (L2218-2224, CHF 6.1bn, 100% coverage). PCAF methodology (L941-948). Not covered: project finance, motor vehicle.

Data Quality Score.evidence: [Verified from full reading] No single overall PCAF DQS published. Per-sector DQS available in detailed tables: Treasury S1&S2 ranges 1.6-5.0, Mortgage 4.2-5.0, Lending 3.8-5.0 (L1965-1970, L2531, L2616). No weighted average disclosed. Score 0.

Attribution Methodology.evidence: [Verified from full reading, L941-948] PCAF signatory. Uses PCAF (2022) Global GHG Accounting and Reporting Standard Part A (L947). SBTi-validated targets (L148, L887). Third-party platform for calculations (L1950-1952). Covers treasury, mortgage, lending books comprehensively. Includes S3 for listed equities, bonds, and corporate loans. Score 4: full PCAF methods, signatory.

Portfolio Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading] Treasury book: 68% outstanding coverage (L2232). Mortgage book: 97% (L2557). Lending book: 100% corporate loans covered. The 49% at L986 is the SBTi portfolio coverage target (share in companies with SBTi targets), NOT financed emissions coverage. Estimated overall ~70% but not explicitly published as a single figure. Conservative score 2.

Temporal Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading] Financed emissions tracked from 2021 baseline (L895). Data for 2021, 2024, 2025 (restated via new platform, L1953). 52% reduction vs 2021 (L172). SBTi targets: 36%+ by 2025 (achieved 49%), 100% by 2040, net-zero by 2050 (L876-883). Score 3.

Attribution Methodology.evidence: [Verified from full reading] PCAF signatory. Uses PCAF Global Standard since 2019 for loan portfolio (L3872). Trucost for investment portfolios. PACTA for sector alignment. White Paper sectoral targets with scenario-based benchmarks (L4043-4048). Dedicated Data and Metrics project within Sustainable Finance Programme (L3809). Score 4: comprehensive PCAF methodology, signatory.

Portfolio Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading] Climate targets cover ~62% of total financed S1&S2 emissions (L3986/L4000). White Paper sectors = 78% of financed S1&S2, 66% of total loan portfolio (L3986). The 44% at L4777 refers to real estate's share of total loan exposure (91bn/207bn), NOT portfolio coverage. PCAF applied to entire loan portfolio since 2021 (L3872). Score 3 (60-80%).

Temporal Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading] PCAF financed emissions since 2019 (L3872), entire portfolio since 2021. 2021 baselines for all White Paper targets. 2030 and 2050 targets across all sectors (L3986-3989). Fourth consecutive progress report (L3877). Score 3.

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading, L5758-5759] Facilitated emissions explicitly immaterial. Verbatim: 'the portion of our portfolio subject to the PCAF Facilitated Emissions Standard relative to the outstanding exposure in our loan portfolio is only 0.46%. We therefore consider this to be immaterial and do not calculate nor publish the facilitated emissions.' Score 0.

Attribution Methodology.evidence: [Verified] No facilitated emissions methodology — immaterial portfolio.

Commercial lines: 173,847 tCO2e with sector breakdown (L5729-5747). Two of four PCAF Part C branches covered (motor S1&S2 + commercial S1&S2). 5.0/8.0 = 62.5%. Described as pilot phase. Score 3.

Temporal Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading] Second year of IAE calculation (first in 2023, L5726). Two years of data. Score 1.

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: [Verified from full reading of Climate and Nature Report 2024, 3236 lines] 9.0/18 = 50.0%. Bonds 90% of portfolio (Table 4, L1126-1130, £87.2bn incl. lifetime mortgages £5.9bn). Property 9% (L1131-1135, £6.0bn). Equity 1% (L1136-1140, £2.9bn). Private debt and private equity covered via proxy (L1190). Sovereign (Government 16%, Table 5 L1172-1176). All S1&S2 only (L1079). CRE uses whole-building data. Motor vehicle not covered.

Data Quality Score.evidence: [Verified from Climate and Nature Report 2024, Table 3 L1108-1111] PCAF DQS = 2.3 (2024), 2.5 (2023). Per asset class (Table 4): Bond 2.3, Property 2.3, Equity 2.3. Score 4: DQS 2.3 falls in 1.5-2.5 range. Auto-scan found 2.8 which was a previous year or different metric.

Attribution Methodology.evidence: [Verified from Climate and Nature Report 2024] PCAF signatory. Reports PCAF DQS across all asset classes (Tables 3-5). Uses EVIC for corporates, sovereign capital stock for sovereigns, market valuations for real estate (L1081-1083). SBTi-validated targets. NZAOA member. Comprehensive financed emissions methodology covering £96.1bn. Score 4: full PCAF methods, signatory, reported status.

Portfolio Coverage.evidence: [Verified from Climate and Nature Report 2024] £96.1bn of £97.6bn proprietary assets included in financed emissions = 98.5% (L1086+L1188). Total absolute emissions: 4.9M tCO2e (L1144). Proxy approaches fill coverage gaps for real estate, lifetime mortgages, private debt, private equity (L1190). Score 5 (>95%).

Temporal Coverage.evidence: [Verified from Climate and Nature Report 2024] Investment GHG intensity tracked from 2019 base year. 37% reduction achieved by 2024 (Table 3, L1102-1104). SBTi targets: 50% reduction by 2030 (L869). Net zero by 2050 (L541). Score 3.
